hawkish_phrases: further gradual increases in the target range for the federal funds rate will be consistent with sustained expansion
it would likely soon be appropriate to take another step in removing policy accommodation
inflation, measured on a 12-month basis, was likely to move modestly above the Committee's objective for a time
a prolonged period in which the economy operated beyond potential could give rise to inflationary pressures
Many participants noted that it would likely be appropriate in the not-too-distant future to revise the Committee's characterization of the stance of monetary policy
dovish_phrases: the stance of monetary policy remains accommodative
some participants suggested there could still be some labor market slack
measures of aggregate nominal wage growth had so far picked up only modestly
uncertainty about the appropriate level of the federal funds rate over the longer run
all participants pointed to ongoing trade disagreements and proposed trade measures as an important source of uncertainty and risks
summary: The Committee unanimously held the target range at 1.75-2 percent with a clear signal that further gradual increases would likely soon be appropriate, maintaining language that policy remains accommodative while noting strong economic activity and inflation near 2 percent.
